items.headline: Tech-Driven Rally Pushes S&P 500 and Nasdaq to Records; Dow Dips Amid Mixed Sentiment
items.summary: The U.S. stock market experienced a mixed but generally positive session on Friday, May 1, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching new record closing highs. Robust corporate earnings, particularly from technology giants, and a slight dip in crude oil prices fueled the gains. However, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ended modestly lower, reflecting broader market hesitancy. Overall market sentiment is navigating strong earnings against the backdrop of rising inflation concerns due to geopolitical tensions and the start of a historically weaker six-month period for stocks. This information is for journalistic purposes only and not financial advice.

items.headline: Apple Leads Gains on Strong iPhone 17 Demand
items.summary: Apple shares advanced significantly after the company provided a solid sales forecast, touting strong demand for its flagship iPhone 17 and the MacBook Neo. The company reported fiscal Q2 2026 revenue up 17% year-over-year.

items.headline: Reddit Jumps on Upbeat Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Reddit's stock jumped following an upbeat quarterly revenue forecast, signaling positive investor sentiment.

items.headline: Roblox Falls After Cutting Bookings Forecast
items.summary: Roblox experienced a sharp decline after the company cut its annual bookings forecast, disappointing investors.

items.headline: Amgen Drops on Modest Guidance
items.summary: Amgen's stock dropped despite beating earnings estimates, as its strong results were overshadowed by modest guidance targets.

items.headline: Exxon Mobil Down as Middle East Disruptions Hit Profit
items.summary: Exxon Mobil shares dipped after the company's quarterly profit was negatively impacted by Middle East disruptions.

items.headline: Fed Sees Historic Dissents Amid Persistent Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark rate steady at 3.50% to 3.75% at its April 2026 meeting, but the decision saw historic dissents, with four of 12 FOMC members expressing concerns about inflation fueled by energy shocks from the Iran war. Some officials urged signaling potential rate hikes, not just cuts, highlighting increased economic uncertainty. Outgoing Chair Jerome Powell indicated the central bank would likely remain on hold for months.

items.headline: S&P 500 Companies Report Strong Q1 Earnings Growth
items.summary: First-quarter earnings season concluded with S&P 500 companies reporting an aggregate earnings growth of 27.8% year-on-year. This marks the biggest earnings growth since Q4 2021, driven by robust performance from major companies, including several in the 'Magnificent Seven' group.

items.headline: Amazon's AWS Segment Continues Strong Growth
items.summary: Amazon reported a 17% increase in first-quarter net sales to $181.5 billion. Its Amazon Web Services (AWS) segment demonstrated particularly strong growth, with sales increasing 28% year-over-year, marking its fastest growth in 15 quarters.

items.headline: Technology Leads Gains, Energy Lags Among Sectors
items.summary: Technology stocks recorded the biggest percentage gains in the recent session, benefiting from strong earnings and AI-driven productivity expectations. Conversely, energy shares dropped the most, impacted by global disruptions and a dip in crude prices. Consumer Staples and Materials sectors are also noted for their resilience.
